title: Rajmahal, Mehsana
tagline: Rajmahal, Mehsana — heritage city of Gujarat

overview.short: Rajmahal is a palace in Mehsana, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: Rajmahal is a palace in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. Built in 1904 by Sayajirao Gaekwad III of Baroda State, it was used as the government office and later as the court until 2017. It has three floors and 130 rooms.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sardar Patel Stadium, Mehsana
topPlaces.description: Sardar Patel Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium and sports complex at Municipal Ground, Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was built at the total cost of ₹11 crore (US$1.2 million) and opened in October 2022.

topPlaces.name: Biladi Baug
topPlaces.description: Biladi Baug, officially known as Mahatma Gandhi Garden, is an urban park in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was established in 1946 and is being redeveloped in 2025 by the Mahesana Municipal Corporation.

topPlaces.name: Para Lake
topPlaces.description: Para Lake, officially Swami Vivekanand Lake, is a man-made lake located in Mehsana city in the Indian state of Gujarat. Excavated during the Gaekwad rule, it was redeveloped and opened in 2019.

topPlaces.name: Boter Kothani Vav
topPlaces.description: Boter Kothani Vav, also known as Mehsana Vav or Interi Vav, is a stepwell located in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was completed in 1674 and was renovated in 2025.

topPlaces.name: Varetha
topPlaces.description: Varetha is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Sangathala
topPlaces.description: Sangathala is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Dabhoda, Mehsana
topPlaces.description: Dabhoda is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Gorisana
topPlaces.description: Gorisana is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Sardar Patel Stadium, Mehsana & around

items.note: Sardar Patel Stadium is a multi-purpose stadium and sports complex at Municipal Ground, Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was built at the total cost of ₹11 crore (US$1.2 million) and opened in October 2022.
Biladi Baug, officially known as Mahatma Gandhi Garden, is an urban park in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was established in 1946 and is being redeveloped in 2025 by the Mahesana Municipal Corporation.
Para Lake, officially Swami Vivekanand Lake, is a man-made lake located in Mehsana city in the Indian state of Gujarat. Excavated during the Gaekwad rule, it was redeveloped and opened in 2019.
Boter Kothani Vav, also known as Mehsana Vav or Interi Vav, is a stepwell located in Mehsana, Gujarat, India. It was completed in 1674 and was renovated in 2025.
Varetha is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.
Sangathala is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.
Dabhoda is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.
Gorisana is a village in Kheralu Taluka in Mahesana district of Gujarat, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rajmahal, Mehsana?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rajmahal, Mehsana is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rajmahal, Mehsana?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (64 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rajmahal, Mehsana?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rajmahal, Mehsana at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rajmahal, Mehsana?
faq.a: Stays in Rajmahal, Mehsana start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rajmahal, Mehsana?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sardar Patel Stadium, Mehsana, Biladi Baug, Para Lake, Boter Kothani Vav, Varetha.
